title: Windcrest
name: Windcrest
metaTitle: Windcrest 1031 Exchange Services in San Antonio, TX
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Windcrest investors weighing I-35 corridor retail, older multifamily, and light-industrial replacement property.

sections.html: Windcrest is a small, independently incorporated city inside Loop 410 on San Antonio's northeast side, wedged between I-35 and Fort Sam Houston, with a commercial base that grew up around 1960s and 1970s residential development and has since aged alongside it. An investor exchanging out of a Windcrest property is usually dealing with older construction across retail, multifamily, and light-industrial alike, and the vintage of the surrounding building stock should factor into every stage of a replacement search here.

sections.heading: Retail Along the I-35 and Walzem Corridor
sections.html:
Strip retail along Walzem Road and the I-35 frontage serves a mostly local, older residential base rather than the regional draw of newer power centers elsewhere in the metro, which keeps rents modest but occupancy relatively stable. Several centers here were built before current parking and access ordinances took effect, so a zoning verification letter is worth pulling before assuming a proposed use is permitted as-of-right.

Proximity to Fort Sam Houston and the AT&T Center adds event-driven traffic on specific nights, but that traffic does not translate into steady daily demand the way base employment does in other San Antonio submarkets, and a lender will generally underwrite Windcrest retail on its baseline local traffic rather than event spillover.

sections.heading: Aging Multifamily Stock and Renovation Exposure
sections.html:
Multifamily product in Windcrest skews toward garden-style complexes built decades ago, many of which have gone through at least one prior renovation cycle. An investor identifying a Windcrest multifamily replacement should request capital improvement records going back further than a typical five-year file, since deferred items from an earlier renovation sometimes resurface as a current property-condition issue.

Unit mix in these complexes also tends to run smaller than newer suburban product, which can mean lower per-unit rents but also lower turnover cost, and an appraiser should be asked to reflect that trade-off directly rather than applying a blanket rent comparable from a newer Windcrest-adjacent submarket.

sections.heading: What Trades in a Windcrest Search
sections.html:
The realistic replacement categories in this submarket are limited but active:

  • strip retail along Walzem Road and the I-35 frontage
  • garden-style multifamily built before 1990
  • small light-industrial and flex space near Rittiman Road
  • service commercial serving Fort Sam Houston-adjacent traffic

sections.heading: Title and Code Review on Pre-1970s Parcels
sections.html:
Because Windcrest incorporated separately from San Antonio, some older parcels carry permit and code-compliance records held by the small city's own building department rather than the county, and those records are not always as complete as an investor expects. A title review should confirm current easements for shared parking between adjacent retail buildings, and a code-compliance letter should be requested directly from Windcrest's building official rather than assumed from the county's file.

A survey is also worth ordering on any Windcrest parcel that has changed hands more than once since the 1970s, since lot lines recorded decades ago do not always match current fencing or parking layouts, and an unresolved boundary discrepancy can complicate both financing and the identification paperwork if it surfaces late.

sections.heading: Managing a Compressed Window in an Older Submarket
sections.html: Given the age of Windcrest's building stock, we start property-condition assessments and lender underwriting as early as possible once a candidate is identified, since older mechanical and roof systems can turn up findings that change a lender's terms mid-process. Investors here often use the 200 percent rule rather than the three-property rule, listing several smaller Windcrest candidates whose combined value stays under twice the relinquished property's value, since no single Windcrest building is likely to match a larger sale on its own.

faqs.q: Why does older construction matter more in Windcrest than in newer San Antonio submarkets?
faqs.a: Most retail and multifamily stock here predates current parking, access, and building codes, so a code-compliance review and property-condition assessment should happen early rather than late in the 45-day identification window.

faqs.q: Does Windcrest's separate city government affect closing documentation?
faqs.a: It can. Permit and code records are held by Windcrest's own building department rather than the county, so a compliance letter should be requested directly from that office instead of relying on county files alone.

faqs.q: What is the 200 percent rule and why would it apply to a Windcrest exchange?
faqs.a: It allows identification of any number of replacement properties as long as their combined value does not exceed 200 percent of the relinquished property's value. Since no single Windcrest building typically matches a larger sale, investors often list several smaller candidates under this rule instead of the three-property rule.

faqs.q: Is retail near Fort Sam Houston and the AT&T Center a stronger investment than typical strip retail?
faqs.a: Event nights add traffic, but lenders generally underwrite these properties on baseline local demand rather than event spillover, so an investor should not overweight game-day or event-driven revenue when modeling returns.

faqs.q: Who confirms my exchange qualifies as like-kind if I'm replacing an older Windcrest building with newer construction?
faqs.a: Real property held for investment or business use qualifies as like-kind to other real property regardless of age or condition, but your qualified intermediary and CPA should still confirm the specific asset and holding period before it goes on your identification list.

faqs.q: Should I order a survey before identifying an older Windcrest parcel?
faqs.a: Yes, particularly if the property has changed hands more than once since the 1970s. Lot lines recorded decades ago do not always match current fencing or parking layouts, and an unresolved boundary discrepancy can delay both financing and closing.
